skill.name: cc-skill-continuous-learning
skill.description: Adds a Claude Code stop hook that identifies reusable lessons from longer coding sessions.
skill.summary: Capture reusable coding lessons from Claude Code sessions.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly true for local filesystem and shell-hook behavior, but no malicious exfiltration or prompt injection was found. The main risk is that the hook can encourage session review and local skill persistence under the user's Claude directory, which may retain sensitive project context.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Learned Skill Storage
medium_findings.description: The skill configures and creates a learned-skills directory under the user's Claude home path. This is legitimate for a continuous-learning workflow, but it can persist derived project or session knowledge outside the active workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The home-directory path and directory creation are explicit. The risk is contextual because local persistence is the skill's intended behavior, and no network exfiltration is present.

medium_findings.title: Session Review Hook May Retain Sensitive Context
medium_findings.description: The stop hook reads the Claude transcript path for message counting and emits instructions to evaluate longer sessions for reusable patterns. This can lead to local retention of sensitive session details if the assistant follows the hook guidance without user review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script only counts transcript messages, but its output directs Claude to evaluate the session and save learned skills. The privacy risk depends on the user's session content and review process.

low_findings.title: Local Shell Utilities Used in Hook Script
low_findings.description: The shell invocation, command substitution, jq, sed, grep, and stderr redirection are local workflow plumbing. They do not show command injection because arguments are derived from the script path, config file, or Claude-provided transcript path and are quoted.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected shell patterns are real, but the command arguments are quoted and there is no eval or dynamic execution. The remaining concern is normal hook-script execution risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md line flagged by static analysis contains only the skill description text. No cryptographic operation, hashing algorithm, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is plain metadata text. There is no code path or cryptographic primitive to evaluate.

low_findings.title: Hidden Claude Directory References Are Intentional
low_findings.description: References to ~/.claude in comments, configuration, and hook paths are expected for a Claude Code skill. They are not hidden malware indicators by themselves, but users should know that the skill operates in their Claude home directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden-directory references match the documented Claude Code hook and skill layout. They are still relevant because they affect files outside the current project.

dangerous_patterns.title: Home Directory Filesystem Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The script resolves the learned-skills path under the user's home directory and creates it if needed. This is not malicious, but marketplace users should understand the persistence behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem write is explicit and confirmed. The intent appears aligned with the skill purpose, so the pattern is a warning rather than a block.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hook Output Influences Assistant Behavior
dangerous_patterns.description: The script emits instructions asking Claude to evaluate sessions and save learned skills. This is the core feature, but it can change assistant behavior at session end.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hook message is directly visible and action-oriented. It does not contain override language or impersonate system instructions.

content.user_title: Build Continuous Learning for Claude Code
content.value_statement: Developer sessions often contain fixes and debugging patterns that are lost after the work ends. This skill adds a Claude Code stop hook that flags longer sessions for review and local lesson capture.
actual_capabilities: Provides a stop-hook shell script for Claude Code sessions.
Counts user messages in the Claude transcript before triggering review.
Loads configurable session length and learned-skill path settings.
Creates a local learned-skills directory when needed.
Signals Claude to evaluate longer sessions for reusable patterns.
limitations: Does not generate learned skills by itself.
Requires jq, grep, sed, bash, and Claude Code hook support.
Only checks session length before requesting review.
Can persist sensitive context if users save lessons without review.

use_cases.title: Preserve Debugging Lessons
use_cases.description: Capture recurring debugging techniques from longer Claude Code sessions for future local reuse.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Learning
use_cases.description: Use a shared review threshold and output location for developers who maintain local Claude Code workflows.

use_cases.title: Review Project-Specific Patterns
use_cases.description: Identify workarounds, project conventions, and repeated fixes after substantial implementation sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Install the Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up this continuous-learning hook for my Claude Code environment and explain which files it will create or modify.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the current session for reusable debugging or project-specific lessons. Ask before saving anything to learned skills.

prompt_templates.title: Tune the Threshold
prompt_templates.prompt: Adjust the continuous-learning configuration for a team that wants review only after substantial coding sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Learned Lessons
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the learned-skill output plan for sensitive data, narrow scope, and duplicate lessons before approving persistence.

output_examples.input: A long debugging session ends after several failed build attempts.
output_examples.output: Claude reports that the session is long enough for review and proposes reusable debugging lessons before local saving.

output_examples.input: A short session contains only a small typo fix.
output_examples.output: The hook skips review because the message count is below the configured threshold.

output_examples.input: A maintainer changes the learned skill path in the config file.
output_examples.output: The hook resolves the configured path and prepares that local directory for future learned skills.

best_practices: Review proposed learned skills before saving them.
Exclude credentials, customer data, and private repository details from retained lessons.
Set a higher session threshold for teams with sensitive projects.
anti_patterns: Do not auto-save lessons from every session.
Do not store secrets or proprietary incident details in learned skills.
Do not enable the hook without documenting local filesystem changes.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to a server?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Does it read Claude transcripts?
faq.answer: The script uses the Claude transcript path to count user messages. It then asks Claude to review longer sessions.

faq.question: Where are learned skills stored?
faq.answer: The default path is under the user's Claude home directory, configured as ~/.claude/skills/learned/.

faq.question: Can users change the review threshold?
faq.answer: Yes. The config file includes a minimum session length setting.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review every proposed learned skill and remove sensitive project details before saving.

faq.question: Does it work outside Claude Code?
faq.answer: It is designed for Claude Code hook behavior and expects Claude Code transcript environment variables.
